structured_payload_candidate.title: Dynamic Glazing Orientation Applicability
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades that do not face north; it works well on east and west orientations and on unshaded south facades.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   1 1 3
D yn a m i c  G l a zi n g
G E N E R AL D E S C R I P TI O N
Dynamic ar chitectural glazing uses electrochromic technology to
dar k en the interior of  a piece of  glass,  enabling a thin coating to change
f r om clear to tinted when small electr ical voltage is applied.  A demon-
str ation of  this technology is eyeglasses that darken when one goes out-
doors.  Dynamic glass f or architectural applications can tr ansf or m into
f our  dark ness levels d epend ing on the season,  being dar ker  in summer
to keep solar gain out and minimize glare .  In ad dition,  because the
glazing becomes d ark er  in the summer when exposed  to sun,  its use
will generally minimize r adiation heat tr ansf er and help impr ove ther -
mal comf ort in the space, .  Typically,  the tint corr espond s to 4%,  20%,
40%,  and  60% visible light transmittance T
vis  d epending on manuf ac-
turers.
judge_reason: The evidence provided is from a Dynamic Glazing section that does not mention orientation applicability; only a later section in the evidence for cand_bd0e4420eabb5cfa contains orientation guidance, so this candidate lacks supporting evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.process: Desiccant rotation between process and reactivation airstreams
structured_payload_candidate.title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Airflow Operation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In rotary desiccant dehumidifiers, humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while a separate reactivation air stream dries another part; the desiccant rotates slowly between these airstreams.

evidence_text:
AS H R AE  G R E E N G U I D E   22 5
Desic c a n t Cool in g
a n d  D eh u m id ific a tion
GE N E RAL D E SCRI P TI O N
Ther e are two basic types of  open-cycle desiccant process:  solid  and
liq uid  desiccant.  Each of  these pr ocesses has several f orms,  and these
should be investigated to deter mine the most appropriate f or  the partic-
ular application.  B oth of  these systems need to have the air  being con-
ditioned  come in good contact with the d esiccant,  during which
moisture is absor bed  f rom the air and the temper atures of  both the air
and  d esiccant are coincid ently raised .
The moistur e absorption process is caused by desiccant having a
lower surf ace vapor pr essur e than the air.  As the temperature of  the
desiccant rises,  its vapor pressure r ises,  and its usef ul absorption capa-
bility lessens.  S ome systems,  particular ly liq uid  types,  have cooling of
air and desiccant coincident with dehumidif ication.
judge_reason: The evidence provided only describes general desiccant processes and does not mention rotary rotation or separate airstreams; the candidate's claim is not supported by the cited pages.

structured_payload_candidate.performance_characteristic: Outlet temperature rise proportional to moisture removal
structured_payload_candidate.title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Process Air Outlet Temperature Rise
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The process air outlet temperature from a rotary desiccant dehumidifier rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture removed, primarily due to heat of sorption.

structured_payload_candidate.application_examples: Hygroscopic materials, film drying, food manufacturing, drug/chemical/plastic/electronics manufacturing, moisture-sensitive packaging, supermarkets
structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Dehumidification Application Examples
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Candidates for active desiccant dehumidification include facilities handling hygroscopic materials; film drying; candy, chocolate, chewing gum, drug, chemical, plastic, and electronics manufacturing; moisture-sensitive product packaging; and supermarkets to avoid condensation on refrigerated casework.

structured_payload_candidate.strategy: Higher temps for drier air, lower temps acceptable for less stringent humidity requirements
structured_payload_candidate.title: Desiccant Reactivation Temperature Strategy
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For rotary desiccant dehumidifiers, higher reactivation air inlet temperatures are generally used when drier outlet air is needed, while low-grade heat sources (e.g., waste heat) can be used when leaving humidity need not be especially low.
